name: GULF DISTRIBUTING
tagline: From one struggling Mobile beer house to the Gulf Coast's beverage powerhouse

lede.hq: 401 N. Water Street, Mobile, Alabama (restored Press-Register building)
lede.industry: Beer, energy drink, wine & spirits wholesale distribution
lede.coverage: Alabama (62 of 67 counties), 10 Florida Panhandle counties, and 19 southern/central Mississippi counties
lede.size: ~1,200-1,300 employees; 100+ suppliers; 1,000+ brands; ~23 million cases/year across 10,000+ retail accounts
lede.narrative: Gulf Distributing was founded on December 19, 1973, when Freida G. Maisel — a Mobile native and 25-year schoolteacher — bought the assets of the failing Jax Distributing Company, becoming the first woman to outright own (not inherit) an Alabama beer distributorship. Starting with 15 employees and three brands, she grew it into a regional institution, famously bringing Coors across the Mississippi River and Red Bull into Alabama. Her son Elliot B. Maisel took the helm in the early 1990s and scaled the company through a string of mergers — Goldring Gulf (2001, Florida), Allstate Beverage (2009, Montgomery), and the Schilleci-family Birmingham and Huntsville expansions (2017/2020) — assembling six operating companies under Gulf Distributing Holdings. A third Maisel generation (Louis, Rebecca, Evan) now sits in the C-suite, and in October 2025 the company consolidated its corporate and distribution operations into a $60 million, 243,000-sq-ft renovated Press-Register building in downtown Mobile.

facts.label: Founded by Freida G. Maisel (bought Jax Distributing)
Employees across AL, FL Panhandle & MS
New downtown Mobile HQ in restored Press-Register building

timeline.title: Jax Distributing predecessor
timeline.body: Jax Distributing Company — the beer wholesaler whose assets Freida Maisel would later buy — traces its Mobile roots to the mid-1930s.

timeline.title: Gulf Distributing founded
timeline.body: On December 19, 1973, former schoolteacher Freida G. Maisel purchases the struggling Jax Distributing operation, renames it Gulf Distributing Company, and starts with 15 employees and three brands — becoming the first woman to outright own an Alabama beer distributorship.

timeline.title: One million cases
timeline.body: Gulf reaches the one-million-case sales milestone just four years after founding.

timeline.title: First Miller Masters award
timeline.body: Gulf wins Miller Brewing Company's prestigious Miller Masters Award for distribution excellence — the first of many industry honors.

timeline.title: Coors crosses the river
timeline.body: Freida Maisel secures Coors distribution for the Mobile area, bringing the brand across the Mississippi River into Alabama.

timeline.title: Elliot Maisel takes over
timeline.body: Freida retires at age 70 and hands management to her son Elliot B. Maisel, who becomes Chairman & CEO and begins an aggressive growth-by-acquisition strategy.

timeline.title: Red Bull / Energy Beverage
timeline.body: Gulf partners with Red Bull North America — the first energy beverage in Alabama — and founds Energy Beverage Management to represent Red Bull across 18 Mississippi counties.

timeline.title: Goldring Gulf merger (Florida)
timeline.body: Goldring Gulf Distributing, LLC forms in July 2001 via merger of Gulf Distributing Company of Florida and N. Goldring; it now runs a 192,000-sq-ft Santa Rosa County, FL facility with 350+ employees.

timeline.title: Allstate Beverage acquired
timeline.body: Allstate Beverage Company, LLC of Montgomery becomes a subsidiary of Gulf Distributing Holdings on January 1, 2009, extending Gulf into central Alabama.

timeline.title: Birmingham JV with Schilleci family
timeline.body: Gulf Distributing Co. of Birmingham is formed as a joint venture with the Schilleci family, adding the Birmingham and Tuscaloosa territories.

timeline.title: Spirits added
timeline.body: Gulf expands beyond beer and energy into liquor, adding spirits brands (including Murder Creek) to its portfolio.

timeline.title: Supreme Beverage / Huntsville
timeline.body: On December 4, 2020, Gulf closes on Supreme Beverage Co. of Huntsville, adding 18 north-Alabama counties, 4.5M cases and 200 employees; principals Michael, Joseph and Phillip Schilleci become Gulf Holdings stakeholders. Gulf now serves 62 of 67 Alabama counties.

timeline.title: NBWA Trailblazer Award named for Freida
timeline.body: The National Beer Wholesalers Association establishes the Freida G. Maisel Trailblazer Award, honoring the founder's pioneering legacy.

timeline.title: 50th anniversary & founder's passing
timeline.body: Gulf marks its 50th anniversary; founder Freida G. Maisel dies at home in Mobile on February 26, 2023, at age 95. She is posthumously honored with the inaugural Freida G. Maisel Businesswoman of the Year Award.

timeline.title: Elliot Maisel named Alabama CEO of the Year
timeline.body: Business Alabama names Elliot B. Maisel the state's 2024 CEO of the Year at its awards luncheon in Birmingham (March 1, 2024).

timeline.title: $5M gift to USA medical school
timeline.body: Elliot Maisel gifts $5 million to the University of South Alabama's Whiddon College of Medicine — the largest individual donation to its new $200M building — with a gathering space named the Maisel Family Forum.

timeline.title: New downtown Mobile HQ opens
timeline.body: In October 2025 Gulf cuts the ribbon on its $60 million, 243,000-sq-ft headquarters in the restored Press-Register building at 401 N. Water Street, featuring a Cirrus Tech automated storage/retrieval crane, AI load recognition and pallet microchip tracking.

news.headline: Gulf Distributing opens new $60 million headquarters in downtown Mobile
news.summary: Gulf cut the ribbon on its restored 243,000-sq-ft Press-Register building HQ, calling it one of the Southeast's most advanced beverage distribution centers, with AI load recognition and an automated storage/retrieval crane.

news.headline: Community invited to 'Beers & Cheers on Dauphin Street' for downtown move
news.summary: Gulf invited the public to celebrate its move downtown with a ribbon-cutting alongside the Mobile Chamber and HQ tours; the company also donated $10,000 to neighboring Calloway-Smith Middle School.

news.headline: Elliot B. Maisel gifts $5 million to Whiddon College of Medicine
news.summary: Gulf's chairman/CEO made the largest individual gift to the University of South Alabama's new $200M medical school building; a gathering area will be named the Maisel Family Forum.

news.headline: Gulf Distributing CEO Elliot B. Maisel named Alabama's CEO of the Year
news.summary: Business Alabama named Maisel the state's top executive for 2024 from five finalists, citing decades of growth via supplier partnerships and acquisitions.

news.headline: Obituary: Freida Gutlow Maisel, founder, dies at 95
news.summary: Freida G. Maisel, who in 1973 bought a small Mobile wholesaler and built it into a multi-state operation employing nearly 2,000, died at home at age 95. She was an NBWA Life Service Award winner (2009).

news.headline: Gulf Distributing to relocate operations to former Press-Register building
news.summary: Gulf announced it would relocate its operations to the former Press-Register building in downtown Mobile, anticipating bringing hundreds of employees downtown as part of a multimillion-dollar investment.

highlights: First energy beverage in Alabama via Red Bull partnership (2000)
Brought Coors across the Mississippi River to Mobile (mid-1980s)
Expanded from 3 brands at founding to 1,000+ today across 100+ suppliers
Added wine and spirits categories (2019) beyond beer and energy
Won Miller Brewing's Miller Masters award beginning 1978

facilities.name: Gulf Distributing Holdings HQ (Press-Register building)
facilities.note: $60M, 243,000-sq-ft renovated headquarters and advanced distribution hub opened Oct 2025; ASRS crane, AI load recognition, pallet microchip tracking.

facilities.name: Gulf Distributing of Mobile
facilities.note: Original/flagship operating company serving coastal Alabama.

facilities.name: Goldring Gulf Distributing
facilities.note: Formed 2001; 192,000-sq-ft facility, 350+ employees, serves NW Florida panhandle (10 counties).

facilities.name: Allstate Beverage Company
facilities.note: Became a Gulf Holdings subsidiary Jan 1, 2009; covers central Alabama.

facilities.name: Gulf Distributing of Alabama (Birmingham/Tuscaloosa & Huntsville)
facilities.note: Birmingham/Tuscaloosa via 2017 Schilleci JV; Huntsville + 18 north-AL counties via 2020 Supreme Beverage deal.

facilities.name: Energy Beverage Management
facilities.note: Founded 2000; distributes Red Bull across 18 Mississippi counties.

pullQuotes.text: This new headquarters brings us home. It honors our family's roots in Mobile while positioning Gulf to better serve our customers.
pullQuotes.cite: Elliot B. Maisel, Chairman & CEO (2025 HQ opening)

pullQuotes.text: We are excited to expand our distribution footprint into the north part of Alabama. Huntsville is a vibrant city with a rapidly growing economy.
pullQuotes.cite: Elliot B. Maisel on the 2020 Supreme Beverage acquisition

people.name: Freida G. Maisel
people.meta: Bought Jax Distributing in 1973; first woman to outright own an AL beer distributorship; NBWA Life Service Award (2009); died 2023 at 95. Deceased.

people.name: Elliot B. Maisel
people.meta: Freida's son; led since early 1990s; Business Alabama 2024 CEO of the Year; $5M USA medical school gift.

people.name: Rebecca L. Maisel
people.meta: Corporate strategy across the Gulf family of companies.

people.name: Michael Schilleci
people.meta: Supreme Beverage principal; joined Gulf executive committee in 2020 deal.

people.name: Joseph Schilleci
people.meta: Supreme Beverage principal; Gulf Holdings stakeholder (2020).

people.name: Phillip Schilleci
people.meta: Supreme Beverage principal; Gulf Holdings stakeholder (2020).

flags.description: Founder Freida Maisel died in 2023; leadership is consolidated in the Maisel family across a second (Elliot) and third (Louis, Rebecca, Evan) generation, a common private-company key-person concentration.
The Schilleci family became Gulf Holdings stakeholders via the 2017 Birmingham JV and 2020 Supreme Beverage deal, creating a multi-family ownership structure within a privately held LLC.
Recent $60M HQ build plus a $5M philanthropic gift represent large outlays for a private distributor, though backed by ~23M cases/year of volume.

sources.label: Brewbound — Gulf closes on Supreme Beverage deal
NBWA — Elliot Maisel named Alabama CEO of the Year
University of South Alabama — Maisel $5M gift
